title: smart: surface state_evidence on TUI dangling-ball glyph (fix unreliable balls/fish status)
description:
Move: in src/bin/swimmers_tui/render.rs:170-225 (render_balls_theme_ball), make the cord_char and rows pick consult SpriteEvidenceConfidence/StateEvidence so unobserved/cache-fallback states render with a dashed cord (e.g. `'` or `.`) and a `( ? )` ball body, mirroring the existing `busy?` label suffix. Then extend the Glance Test (src/bin/swimmers_tui/tests/, baseline at commit 96d157b) into a golden snapshot covering every (SessionState, RestState, StateEvidence{high,observed,unobserved}) tuple — assert the rendered ASCII bytes for each.

Repo head: 08d7cd5
Compared-to head: 08d7cd5 (prior smart link swimmers-lp4 was workbench-scroll, no implementation yet on either chain)
Modes report: none on file

Repo signals (from 4-agent parallel investigation):
- src/bin/swimmers_tui/render.rs:170-225 cord_char / rows pick is keyed only on SpriteKind, not StateEvidence; the existing `?` suffix lives on the label and never reaches the ball
- src/bin/swimmers_tui/entity.rs:54-68 SpriteKind::from_session collapses (state, rest_state) → 8 kinds with no `Unknown/Stale` variant
- src/session/supervisor.rs:546-562 collect_live_summaries silently returns last cached summary (state may be 30s+ old) on tokio::time::timeout(2s), only flipping state_evidence to unobserved("summary_cache_overloaded") — Rank 1 root cause
- src/scroll/guard.rs:115-128 ScrollGuard buffers up to COALESCE_MS=32ms BEFORE state detection; ≥10 CSI sequences trigger buffering. 0 integration tests with detector → Rank 2 root cause for state flap
- src/session/supervisor.rs:322-326 every persisted-session reload forces state=Exited until first detector tick, 0.5–2s window where every ball renders as exited
- Glance Test harness at commit 96d157b only verifies (SessionState, RestState) → distinct LABEL; never asserts the rendered cord_char + ball glyph + drop distance bytes
- src/web/app.css:1907-1919 .is-quiet and .is-working both fill #5379a8 (identical blue) — separate web-side collision, parallel symptom
- src/web/app.js:4225 trogdorAgentGlyph, :4213 trogdorDomReason, :4091-4178 renderTrogdorAgent, :3928 renderTrogdorStructure, :2652 trogdorClawgDismissed — all completely uncovered by app_behavior.test.mjs

Integrity gap: the visual sprite mapping itself is correct, but the system silently shows confident-looking balls for stale/unobserved state. The information needed to flag uncertainty already flows through (StateEvidence → label `?` suffix) but never reaches the ball glyph. This is a missing visual encoding bug AND a missing test bug at once.

Hardening decision: hardening-first. The user explicitly cannot tell which dangling balls are trustworthy; the next move that unblocks every other status improvement is to make StateEvidence visible on the ball, then lock that in with a golden snapshot per (state, rest_state, evidence) tuple.

CRAP status: src/bin/swimmers_tui and src/state/detector are rust-supported but no recent crap baseline captured in this run; declare unknown baseline rather than imply a measurement.

Loop goal: swimmer dangling balls (and fish) visually encode StateEvidence confidence, so an operator instantly distinguishes a confirmed-busy ball from a stale/unobserved-busy ball without hovering for the label.
Success criteria: (a) render_balls_theme renders a visually distinct cord+body for every StateEvidence variant (high/observed-medium/unobserved); (b) golden Glance Test asserts exact rendered ASCII for the full (state, rest_state, evidence) cartesian; (c) operator visual smoke on the live TUI confirms stale vs fresh balls are tellable at a glance.
Loop status: one-shot 1/1
Resume condition: another /smart pass is useful once the rendering change + golden test land and a fresh-eyes pass evaluates whether the supervisor timeout fix (chain:smart task #3) should be the next link.
Completion gate: pending (no implementation yet)

title: Build project-adjacent attention queue manager
description:
Refine the attention group follow-up into a managed attention queue rather than only an auto-open window.

Goal: when more sessions are waiting than fit in the native attention group, choose a coherent cluster by project adjacency and keep the grid flowing one-in-one-out as the operator answers panes.

Candidate behavior:
- Detect local waiting sessions from the same trusted operator-input predicate used by group input and the native attention-group endpoint.
- Score waiting sessions by project adjacency before recency: same repo/client/batch, sibling repos such as `htma` + `htma_server`, and role hints such as frontend/backend/core from directory groups, cwd path, repo names, or overlay metadata.
- Build the visible group as one intentional cluster, not a random top-six queue. Examples: prefer two Sweet Potato sessions together; prefer `htma` frontend plus `htma_server` backend together when both need input; avoid mixing unrelated projects unless the queue has no coherent cluster.
- Preserve the existing manual `[attention group]` button as the deterministic fallback and entry point.
- Auto-refresh the managed `swimmers-attention` tmux group only when explicitly enabled; rate-limit refresh and avoid stealing focus after the initial open.
- Support one-in-one-out rotation: when a visible pane leaves the waiting set after operator input, remove/dim it and swap in the next best adjacent waiting session if available.

Build on the existing native attention-group endpoint, operator-pressure readiness predicate, thought rail grouping, directory group metadata, and tmux handoff code. Do not introduce a separate scheduler or new backend truth source for agent intent.
acceptance_criteria:
- Manual `[attention group]` behavior continues to work and remains a deterministic fallback.
- A project-adjacency scoring helper ranks waiting sessions using existing facts only: cwd/repo path, batch metadata, directory groups/overlay hints when available, tool/session metadata, state/action cues, and recency as a tie-breaker.
- Selection chooses a coherent visible cluster up to the configured cap rather than blindly taking the six most recent sessions.
- Sibling project examples are covered by tests: two Sweet Potato sessions group together; `htma` + `htma_server` are treated as adjacent frontend/backend/core work rather than unrelated sessions.
- One-in-one-out rotation is covered: after a visible session becomes non-waiting, the managed group swaps in the next best eligible session without stealing focus.
- The auto-manager is opt-in, rate-limited, and idempotent; it does not repeatedly focus the native terminal after initial open.
- Remote/namespaced sessions, stale sessions, unhealthy transport, unobserved state evidence, exited sessions, deep sleep, and the managed `swimmers-attention` session itself are excluded.
- HTTP and TUI/in-process semantics remain aligned for any new request/response fields.
- Targeted Rust tests cover scoring, selection, rotation, native tmux refresh behavior, API/in-process parity, and TUI messaging/click behavior.
- Docs/README mention only implemented behavior, including the native attention-group endpoint if it remains public API.
notes:
smart_goal_mode: compact-goal
repo_head: 947f6c2
current_evidence:
- User wants project-adjacent grouping and one-in-one-out rotation rather than a simple auto-refresh of the current attention window.
- Current dirty tree already contains the manual native attention-group slice across API/TUI/native/operator-pressure files.
- Existing open bead `swimmers-d8z` only covered auto-managing the waiting set; this refines the same bead instead of creating a duplicate.
- Prior smart chain says the manual path must remain the base and the auto-manager must build on the existing native endpoint.
smart_goal_contract:
/goal $describe $divide-and-conquer $reproduce $commit process complete

One-shot goal: Turn the current Swimmers attention-group follow-up into a project-adjacent attention queue manager: keep the manual `[attention group]` handoff, add intentional cluster selection for related projects/repos, and implement one-in-one-out pane rotation so answered panes are replaced by the next best waiting session without stealing focus.

Success criteria:
- Start by proving and committing the existing manual attention-group slice, including the tmux target-form fix, so the queue manager builds on a trusted base rather than a broken handoff.
- Write a `/describe` packet for attention queue semantics before implementation: project-adjacency scoring, cluster selection, visible-vs-backlog membership, one-in-one-out rotation, auto-open/focus rules, and fallback/manual behavior.
- Selection uses existing facts only: `SessionSummary` cwd/repo/batch/tool/action-cue fields, directory groups/overlay hints when available, and operator-pressure readiness. Do not invent a parallel scheduler or a separate agent-intent state model.
- The visible group prefers coherent adjacency over raw recency: same project first, sibling frontend/backend/core repos next, then same batch/client/tool context, with recency only as a tie-breaker.
- Add fixtures/tests for the examples that motivated the feature: multiple Sweet Potato waiting sessions group together; `htma` and `htma_server` are treated as adjacent frontend/backend/core work; unrelated sessions do not displace a coherent cluster.
- Implement one-in-one-out rotation: when a visible pane stops waiting after operator input, the managed attention group refreshes in place and swaps in the next best eligible queued session if one exists, without refocusing the native app.
- Preserve safety boundaries: exclude stale/unhealthy/unobserved/exited/deep-sleep/remote/namespaced sessions and the managed `swimmers-attention` session itself.
- Keep API and TUI in-process behavior aligned for new request/response fields; keep docs and README limited to implemented behavior.
- Verification includes targeted Rust tests for scoring/selection/rotation/native refresh, TUI interaction tests, `cargo fmt --check`, and the narrow `cargo test` suites that cover API/native/TUI attention behavior; run broader `cargo test --features personal-workflows` before commit when local capacity allows.
- Finish with `br sync --flush-only`, a coherent commit, and a post-commit reality check of the attention workflow.
- Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped detached reviewer subagent for that gate, plus additional detached review rounds as needed: launch or route an independent reviewer that did not implement the work, have it inspect the final diff/artifacts/validation evidence, and address or explicitly block on its findings before marking the goal complete.

Loop goal: make Swimmers' attention group behave like an intentional operator queue that shows related waiting work together and continuously replaces resolved panes with the next best adjacent item.
Loop status: one-shot 1/1 for goal generation; execution remains pending.
Resume condition: first prove the manual attention group works in the real TUI/Ghostty path, then start the `/describe` packet for adjacency scoring and one-in-one-out rotation.
Completion gate: pending.

title: Keep stale remote fish visible during remote polling failures
description: Remote aggregation currently returns empty sessions on global backoff or target failure, making remote fish disappear. Cache last successful remote summaries per target and mark them stale/degraded during transport failure.
design:
Writes: src/api/remote_sessions.rs, src/types.rs if metadata is extended, tests.
Non-goals: do not build a distributed registry; cache is process-local and best effort.
Model route: Codex gpt-5.5.
acceptance_criteria:
- Remote polling backoff is per target rather than global, or the remaining global behavior is explicitly justified in code/tests.
- Last successful remote summaries are returned during backoff with stale/degraded transport metadata and last_seen/target context.
- The TUI/API can distinguish remote transport failure from stopped remote sessions.
- Validation includes remote_sessions tests for one failed target not hiding other targets and for stale returned summaries during backoff.
notes: This is a concrete dependency-health child: user-visible absence is the regret, stale/degraded rows are the proof.
close_reason: Completed per-target remote polling cache/backoff: stale cached remote summaries remain visible with degraded transport metadata during target failures while healthy targets continue polling. Verified rustfmt --edition 2021 --check src/types.rs src/api/remote_sessions.rs, git diff --check, and cargo test --lib api::remote_sessions::tests:: -- --test-threads=1 (12 passed).

title: smart goal: harden Grok launcher contract
description:
smart_goal_mode: compact-goal
repo_head: 1ac0fe8
local_date: 2026-05-15
prior_chain: swimmers-hme
compared_to: 31520b9
modes_report: unavailable; overlay evaluation root has no swimmers modes report
current_evidence:
- User clarified the issue is the launcher launching things wrong, not generic PATH typing.
- Current dirty tree adds SpawnTool::Grok and makes it the default TUI picker tool.
- Current session spawn command path still falls through to `grok <prompt>`, but local `grok --help` advertises prompt mode as `-p/--single` or `--prompt-file`, not positional prompt text.
- Current dirty commit-helper rewrite calls `grok -p ... --session-id ...`, but local `grok --help` does not support `--session-id`.
- `whence -a grok` resolves /Users/b/.local/bin/grok and /Users/b/.grok/bin/grok, both pointing at the same local binary today, so the immediate blocker is command shape and launcher contract, not only binary lookup.
- Existing worktree has unrelated dirty thought/runtime/web edits; launcher work must stay scoped.
smart_goal_contract:
/goal $describe $reproduce $cli-ergonomics $divide-and-conquer $reality-check-for-project process complete

One-shot goal: Make Swimmers launch Grok through an explicit launcher profile instead of raw `grok <prompt>` or stale Codex-derived flags: regular picker, batch picker, web create, in-process API, and commit-helper launches all resolve the intended binary, use the CLI supported prompt mode, and preserve a usable tmux pane after the first task.

Success criteria:
- Start with a `/describe` pass/fail contract for launcher behavior: no-prompt interactive launch, single-session prompt launch, batch prompt launch, web create, TUI in-process mirror, and `[commit]` helper.
- Reproduce the current failure with command-builder tests or a local harness: `grok "hi"` is treated as an unsupported command shape, and the dirty commit-helper flags include unsupported `--session-id`; record `grok --help` as the local contract (`-p/--single`, `--prompt-file`, `--cwd`, `--always-approve`, `--no-alt-screen`, no `--session-id`).
- Add a small launcher profile abstraction for `SpawnTool` that separates binary resolution, prompt transport, cwd handling, headless-vs-interactive mode, and post-exit shell behavior; do not scatter shell one-liners per surface.
- For Grok, prefer `SWIMMERS_GROK_BIN` when set, otherwise resolve `grok`; support `--prompt-file` or `-p` for initial prompts, pass `--cwd` explicitly when supported, and never use positional prompt arguments or `--session-id` unless a local help check proves support.
- Keep Codex/Claude behavior intentionally unchanged except where the shared profile layer makes existing behavior safer; preserve Codex prompt-file privacy and CAAM fallback semantics.
- Align HTTP API, TUI external client, TUI in-process client, web batch create, and commit-helper behavior so all use the same launcher contract and error messages.
- Add focused tests in `src/session/supervisor.rs`, `src/host_actions.rs`, and `src/bin/swimmers_tui/tests/mod.rs` proving Grok command strings, tool toggling/defaults, batch spawn propagation, and commit-helper invocation.
- Update README/QUICKSTART only for implemented launcher env vars and semantics; do not document Grok wrapper assumptions that are not enforced by code.
- Validate with `cargo test --bin swimmers-tui`, focused `cargo test --lib session::supervisor host_actions types`, `cargo fmt --check`, `git diff --check`, and a local smoke that creates a Grok session from the picker or API without the `hi`/unsupported-command failure.
- Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped reviewer worker that did not implement the work, preferably routed through `/divide-and-conquer`; the reviewer must inspect the final launcher diff, tests, docs, and local smoke evidence before completion.

Loop goal: turn Swimmers agent launching into a dependable local operator contract, so choosing Grok in the picker starts the intended tool with the intended prompt semantics instead of leaking CLI-version assumptions into tmux.
Loop status: one-shot 1/1 for goal generation; execution is pending.
Resume condition: begin with the `/describe` launcher contract and a failing Grok command-builder reproduction, then patch the shared launcher profile and run the named validation gates.
Completion gate: pending for implementation; smart-goal artifact logged in Beads.
close_reason: recommendation delivered

title: Enforce terminal resize bounds at the WebSocket/server boundary
description: Browser JS clamps terminal geometry, but non-browser WebSocket clients can send any u16. Enforce the terminal size contract server-side before PTY resize.
design:
Writes: src/web/mod.rs, src/session/actor.rs, src/types.rs if needed, tests.
Non-goals: do not change normal browser resize UX.
Model route: Codex gpt-5.5.
acceptance_criteria:
- Resize dimensions are clamped or rejected server-side with documented min/max values aligned with the browser surface.
- Zero, one-cell, and huge u16 resize payloads are covered by protocol/actor tests.
- Bad resize payloads cannot create PTY weirdness, noisy logs, or layout state outside the accepted range.
- Validation includes targeted web socket message tests and actor resize tests.
notes: There are existing local edits in src/web/mod.rs at graph-creation time; future workers must inspect current diff before editing.
close_reason: Enforced browser-aligned resize bounds at WebSocket and actor PTY boundaries with focused tests

title: Replace long-lived WebSocket query tokens with safer remote-token auth
description: Remote/token deployments currently put long-lived bearer tokens in WebSocket URLs. Add short-lived WebSocket tickets or first-message auth so URL-bearing surfaces do not expose operator/observer tokens.
design:
Writes: src/web/app.js, src/web/mod.rs, src/auth/mod.rs if shared helpers move, src/types.rs if ticket payloads are added, README.md, tests.
Non-goals: do not add TLS/proxy infrastructure; this is token transport hardening inside the existing server.
Model route: Codex gpt-5.5.
acceptance_criteria:
- Long-lived AUTH_TOKEN/OBSERVER_TOKEN values are no longer placed in browser WebSocket URLs in token mode.
- Operator vs observer scopes remain preserved for WebSocket sessions.
- Ticket or first-message auth failure paths are explicit and tested.
- README documents any residual token-mode caveats for Tailnet/non-loopback use.
- Validation includes web/auth tests and a browser JS smoke or unit-level URL construction check.
notes: Calibrated as P2: not critical for default loopback, but important before treating remote token mode as polished.
close_reason: Implemented first-message WebSocket auth: browser WebSocket URLs no longer carry AUTH_TOKEN/OBSERVER_TOKEN; operator/observer scopes and invalid auth paths covered by cargo web/auth tests and node app behavior tests.
